persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for Chloe, who wants upscale, interesting things to do in Boston.
She loves:
- Art: gallery openings, museum events, art shows, artist talks, art fairs, photography exhibits, sculpture
- Food & drink: wine tastings, wine dinners, chef's table experiences, cocktail classes, food festivals, upscale restaurant events, mixology
- Fancy experiences: galas, benefit dinners, charity auctions, cultural receptions, film premieres, theater openings
- Design, fashion, and aesthetics — runway shows, trunk shows, luxury boutique events, bridal showcases, couture
- Wellness and beauty events that are upscale (spa launches, product launches, brand events)
- Sip-and-craft events (sip & paint, pottery, floral arranging) — she likes these IF the crowd seems upscale or scene-y; skip if it reads as basic/suburban
- Anything cultured, beautiful, or scene-y
- Asian cultural events are great (she is Chinese and appreciates these)

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- "Fashionably LATE" Thursdays at The Liberty Hotel — weekly interactive runway with models weaving through the crowd, free admission, scene-y crowd
- Boston Fashion Week (Oct 2026) events, especially luxury eveningwear, couture, and accessory showcases
- Boston En Vogue — luxury gown and couture runway shows
- Trunk shows at luxury boutiques (Galia Lahav bridal, Agent Provocateur, Newbury St boutiques)
- SwimBoston — annual swimwear/bikini runway at Boston Harbor Distillery
- Any event at a Newbury St boutique, Back Bay gallery, or luxury hotel
- Museum opening receptions with cocktails or wine — Harvard Art Museums, MFA, ICA, Gardner — the "art party" type evening where the crowd is cultured and interesting, not just a museum visit
- University arts/cultural events at Harvard, MIT, BU that are open to the public and have a reception component

Lower priority for her:
- Pure civic/government/policy events (unless they're glamorous)
- Sports
- Basic networking mixers with no food/drink/art angle
- Sip-and-craft events at chain venues or with a generic/tourist vibe (score ≤ 4)

events.name: HEY RIM JEON
events.description: Described as a brilliant pianist by legendary saxophonist Benny Golson, Hey Rim Jeon has been enjoying the recent accolades and acknowledgements of her accomplishments as a musician and educator, following the release of her new jazz recording Groovitude, which soared to No. 14 on the Jazz Week National Radio Chart and remained in the top 20 for several consecutive weeks. The recording features a guest spot by her dear friend and colleague Grammy Award-winning jazz drummer Terri Lyne Carrington, as well as bassist John Lockwood and drummer Yoron Israel. The recording holds a special place in her heart, as its dedicated to her late father. In addition, her recent sold-out CD release concert at Scullers, the No. 1 Jazz Club in Boston, and the world-famous Birdland jazz club, established Hey Rim as a force to be reckoned with in the jazz community. She received rave reviews from fans and music critics, as well as members of the Korean Cultural Center and Korean Cultural Society. A high point of her trip to New York was viewing her image for the first time on a billboard at Times Square that flashed brightly during her gig at Birdland. On the educational side, Hey Rim was granted an early promotion to Professor at the Berklee College of Music, where she has been teaching in the Piano Department since 2010. In 2021, she received Berklees prestigious Exemplary Mentorship Award and is the first Korean faculty member at the institution. Hey Rim recently received the honor of being included in Marquiss Whos Who in America 2022 (75th Edition). As leader of her own band, Hey Rim Jeon and Friends, she has performed both nationally and internationally. Bill Milkowski of JazzTimes described her album Mona Lisa Puzzle as an intense, intelligent statement. It was awarded the Best Jazz Album in the 2009 New England Urban Music Awards, and reached CD Babys Top 10 Modern Creative Jazz Albums list. Hey Rim has appeared as a featured guest on NBCs Weekend Today in New York and was the subject of a documentary on Koreas National TV network titled In the Name of Korea. She has also been interviewed on a variety of podcasts and has performed her work on several online TV and radio shows. ALL TICKETS ARE NON-REFUNDABLE AND CANNOT BE EXCHANGED FOR ANOTHER SHOW.If you require special accommodations, please call the box office at 617-562-4111.
events.reason: Hey Rim Jeon concert — accomplished Korean pianist/professor, jazz excellence, cultural resonance for Chloe, upscale music experience

events.name: Museum of Fine Arts First Fridays With Boston Singles
events.description:
Looking to relax & have fun at the end of a long week? Unwind with MFA First Fridays, an exciting evening of music, dancing, art making, and more on the first Friday of select months. See art, meet new people, try cocktails, delicious bites, & let your hair down as you welcome the weekend at the MFA.

Tickets include general admission to the MFA & are free for members. First Fridays sell out fast, so plan ahead & purchase tickets in advance. Limited time slots are available for art making. Reser
events.reason: MFA First Fridays is a high-energy art museum event with cocktails, music, dancing, and art-making. Upscale venue, cultured crowd, and Chloe loves museum evening receptions with a social scene component.

events.name: TRIAD + CELEBRATES THE PRE-RELEASE OF "HERMETIC" THE MUSIC O HERMETO PASCOAL
events.description: TRIAD+ celebrates the release of Hermetic: The Music of Hermeto Pascoal Live at GBH with a special performance at Scullers Jazz Club, featuring Mark Walker (drums), Oscar Stagnaro (bass), Maxim Lubarsky (piano), and Edmar Coln (saxophones).All GA sections (Main Floor GA & Miles Davis Mezzanine) will be seated by host and are first come, first served (other than where a reserved sign is posted to table). If you require special seating, please call the venue for accommodations no later than 48 hours prior to show date.Please note: If you do not purchase all seats at a table, other customers may be seated with you.If you have any special requirements, please call the box office after purchasing your tickets or at least 72 hours prior to show time.ALL TICKETS PURCHASED ARE NON-REFUNDABLE
events.reason: Jazz performance at Scullers Jazz Club celebrating Hermeto Pascoal; upscale venue, cultured music event with interesting lineup. Fits her appreciation for art and cultural experiences.
